summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env and host info to remote devtunnels endpoint on import
details: The main bundle (dist/index.cjs) executes a top-level function that opens an HTTPS POST to the hardcoded host j5dcw95v-3000.inc1.devtunnels.ms at path /post-d whenever the module is required or imported. The JSON payload includes the installer's entire process.env, process.cwd(), process.version, and process.argv, meaning every environment variable of the importing process — including credentials commonly stored in env (AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, CI secrets, DB passwords) — is shipped to an author-controlled Microsoft devtunnels endpoint. The package presents itself as a lightweight ES6 utility helper; the exfiltration is not part of any documented behavior and fires unconditionally on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
